title: rhus toxicarium
intro: Rhus toxicarium, also known as poison ivy, oak, or sumac, is a plant that contains urushiol, a potent allergenic oil that causes skin irritation in many people. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and fever. The health benefits of Rhus toxicarium include its ability to stimulate the immune system, reduce swelling, and promote healing. Herbal preparations of Rhus toxicarium, such as tinctures and salves, have been used topically to treat skin conditions, while its root and bark have been used in teas and infusions for internal use, although caution should be exercised due to its allergenic properties.
